codeReview.summary: The implementation comprehensively addresses all expected behaviors for the Firecracker VM runtime feature. It adds the vmmanager package with full VM lifecycle control (boot, stop, hibernate, resume, recover), implements repo-built guest images via Nix, extends vmctl with idle/logout lifecycle and epoch tracking for crash recovery, and strictly enforces guest secret isolation. All 69 new tests pass and verify the critical invariants.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker correctly followed the existing pattern of internal-only vmctl endpoints with isInternalCaller() checks, properly updating both architecture.md and environment.md to document the new VM lifecycle behavior.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: internal/vmctl/handlers.go:264-272 — all new lifecycle endpoints (hibernate, resume, recover, logout, idle-check) use isInternalCaller() check; architecture.md lines 206-215 document vmmanager package responsibilities

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker followed the infra-worker skill procedure correctly, running baseline tests, updating Nix configuration, and documenting the Node B deployment requirements without attempting macOS Firecracker validation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoff shows: go test ./... passed, nix flake show passed, git push to remote completed; skillFeedback.followedProcedure is true with no deviations

sharedStateObservations.observation: The services.yaml was properly updated to include VMCTL_IDLE_TIMEOUT for local development, matching the Node B production configuration.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/services.yaml line 28: VMCTL_IDLE_TIMEOUT="30m" added to vmctl service definition

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker correctly identified that the guest-image.nix requires Linux/Nix sandbox considerations and documented this as a non-blocking issue for Node B validation, showing awareness of cross-platform build constraints.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoff.discoveredIssues[1]: 'nix/guest-image.nix expression builds a minimal guest rootfs...may need adjustment...Use pkgs.vmTools or a fakeroot approach'

summary: PASS. The feature implementation is thorough and production-ready. All expected behaviors are covered: (1) Repo-built guest images via Nix (nix/guest-image.nix, flake.nix) with explicit exclusion of provider credentials; (2) Idle/logout lifecycle with HibernateVM, ResumeVM, LogoutVM, and automatic idle checking (VAL-VM-008, VAL-CROSS-116); (3) Unhealthy guest recovery via RecoverVM with epoch increment (VAL-VM-009, VAL-CROSS-117); (4) Guest secret isolation verified through TestManagerBuildFirecrackerConfig_NoSecrets and explicit Nix derivation exclusions (VAL-VM-011); (5) User state preservation across stop/hibernate/resume via persistent directory mounting. The 69 new tests (55 vmctl + 14 vmmanager) provide comprehensive coverage of lifecycle transitions and epoch tracking. The only remaining work is Node B deployment validation of the actual Firecracker/KVM runtime, which the worker correctly identified as requiring Linux hardware and documented appropriately.
